title: Revisiting Deepfake Detection: Chronological Continual Learning and the Limits of Generalization
contributions: Pipeline for simulating deepfake scenarios over extended periods
Focus on data, training, and inference efficiency
problem: Traditional deepfake detection methods struggle with continuously emerging deepfake generators, and existing CL approaches have limitations including non-chronological sequences and lack of real-world scenario simulation.

evaluation.focus: Knowledge retention of past generators and generalization to future generators

findings: Some methods retained knowledge of past generators, but none showed strong generalization to future generators.
availability: Code will be released upon acceptance
